channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: The World Cup final between Lionel Messi’s Argentina and Lamine Yamal’s Spain kicks off soon. Follow our coverage.
item.description: The final, the first in the U.S. in 32 years, will be watched by billions across the globe and feature a first-of-its-kind halftime show with Justin Bieber, Shakira, BTS and Madonna.

item.title: Maine Democrats rally around logger Troy Jackson to replace Graham Platner
item.description: Jackson, a progressive who champions worker rights and Medicare-for-all, has quickly emerged as the favorite to take on Republican Susan Collins in the Senate race in November.

item.title: A tech worker thought she’d found one of the safest careers in the U.S. Then everything changed.
item.description: Waves of layoffs and growing pressure to automate jobs are creating existential panic in the tech industry. Economists say other white-collar workers are next.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: How to stop in-laws from making everyone’s plans without asking?
item.description: In-laws are “lovely people,” but their way of making plans for all without consultation is inconsiderate of this couple’s time.

item.title: Why these three books have kept group chats going for months
item.description: The stories end in talk shows and movie deals. But the suffering is real.

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Play The Washington Post's daily crossword puzzles online for free.

item.title: JD Vance is moving to a wealthy enclave outside D.C. Cue the gossip (and complaints).
item.description: Motorcades and helicopter noise top the list of concerns for residents of this old-money town who see it as an escape from Washington.

item.title: ICE officer’s troubled past renews questions about agency’s vetting practices after fatal shooting in Maine
item.description: Ashley Brouillette said her former spouse, David Brouillette, told her he was the Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer who shot and killed a Colombian immigrant in Maine this week. She and others said Brouillette has a volatile history. The shooting has added to concerns about the Trump administration’s push to quickly hire officers to carry out mass deportations.

item.title: U.S. launches new wave of attacks against Iran in retaliation for deaths of two American service members in Jordan
item.description: The renewed strikes come hours after U.S. Central Command announced Saturday that two service members were killed and another was missing. The attacks were launched in an effort to “degrade Iran’s ability to threaten commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz and swiftly punish Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces who launched attacks against American service members in Jordan last night,” it said.

item.title: An Ohio woman took in two strangers. Then she saw their ‘wanted’ photos.
item.description: In an era of declining trust, Adrienne Behrman, 49, took a chance on a couple she hoped to help.

item.title: Wearers dub Zara’s new wide-leg trousers ‘death pants’
item.description: From start to finish, it takes Zara only two to three weeks to produce a garment because of the company’s vertically integrated supply chain. Testing for clothing typically entails a stationary mannequin or a fit model. But women fall in these pants while walking at a quick pace or going up and down steps, not standing in a studio.

item.title: 2 U.S. service members killed, 1 missing after Iran attack on Jordan, military says
item.description: U.S. Central Command said that four additional service members were evacuated to Jordanian hospitals and have since been discharged, and other personnel evaluated for minor injuries have returned to duty. Iran had launched a wave of attacks on U.S. allies Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain as the U.S. military hit Iran, in the seventh consecutive day of clashes.

item.title: Data centers have united Americans of both parties in a shared hatred
item.description: The intensity and breadth of the data center backlash reveals something bigger: Americans’ disgust with political and economic systems that feel stacked against them, experts and activists say.

item.title: ‘Feeling cheated’ that reality of parenting isn’t as wife promised
item.description: Letter writer agreed to have children only if it wouldn’t affect morning workouts. Two kids in, wife now says that’s too much.

item.title: An Oregon teen spotted a sports relic in the thrift store. It’s about to make him rich.
item.description: Quinn Brown, 19, paid $3 for a vintage uniform worn by one of basketball’s all-time greats. Sotheby’s thinks it could be worth $250,000.

item.title: 7 things a morning headache can reveal about your health
item.description: Sometimes, head pain early in the day means you just slept wrong. Other times, it could be a sign of a health condition.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: Thick wildfire smoke is lingering over the Mid-Atlantic. See how long it will last and where it’s going next.
item.description: Dense wildfire smoke is expected to remain across the Mid-Atlantic through Saturday morning before gradually clearing.

item.title: The death of the stick shift is almost here
item.description: Driving a manual vehicle is a lost art. Hopes of a revival have fallen flat. With the rise of EVs and autonomous vehicles, only a few die-hards are holding on.

item.title: Trump says 278,000 noncitizens are on voting rolls. Experts say that’s wrong.
item.description: The claim came as the Trump administration vowed to withhold funding from states that don’t participate in federal programs purporting to secure elections.

item.title: Taylor Farms removes all iceberg lettuce sourced in central Mexico from U.S. market
item.description: The move comes after investigators identified the company’s shredded iceberg lettuce as a likely source of a multi-state cyclosporiasis outbreak.

item.title: The key to happiness may be embracing the struggle
item.description: A writer and professor who studies happiness said that suffering is part of life. It’s what we do with it that matters.

item.title: Every era gets the ‘Odyssey’ it deserves, screens critic Monica Hesse writes. Christopher Nolan’s is astonishing.
item.description: This is the kind of movie that turns out not to be what you think it’s about, where information is revealed that radically changes your understanding of the story, Hesse writes.

item.title: I find my spouse’s aesthetic unappealing: Carolyn Hax is giving advice
item.description: I find my spouse’s aesthetic unappealing: Carolyn Hax is giving advice

item.title: Richard Branson: Thousands missing. Hundreds in jail. Isn’t the U.S. ‘in charge’ of Venezuela?
item.description: Getting political prisoners freed would demonstrate that Trump's end goals are freedom and democracy.

item.title: FDA raised conflict of interest concerns ahead of new panel’s review of controversial peptides
item.description: That panel will meet to weigh whether the agency should allow Americans to have broader access to seven injectable peptides previously restricted over potential safety concerns.

item.title: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record
item.description: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record

item.title: Chicago, Detroit and D.C. top list for world’s worst air quality as Canadian wildfire smoke arrives. Search to see affected areas.
item.description: A global air quality index showed the early-morning pollution in five major North American cities at higher levels than those next on the list, including Kinshasa in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Nairobi, Kenya.

item.title: Key takeaways from Trump’s prime-time address
item.description: The president largely revived familiar claims about fraud, criticized media outlets and urged passage of a bill that would require proof of citizenship to vote.

item.title: Trump speech. Cyclosporiasis source. Reflecting Pool. Blue Angels. Texas floods. Happiness tips. Old Messi photos. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Trump attacks ABC and NBC for refusing to air election integrity speech, says networks’ licenses should be revoked
item.description: ABC and NBC did not specify why they declined to air the speech, but networks are often loath to grant time to presidents for overtly political purposes.

item.title: Lettuce supplier is identified as potential source of cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has linked the Taylor Farms produce supplied to Taco Bell to the outbreak of a parasitic illness that has sickened thousands in United States, according to two individuals familiar with the investigation.

item.title: Blue Angels flyover that sent umbrellas and chairs toppling on Florida beach won’t lead to punishment, top Navy official says
item.description: “No reprimands. No firings. No problem,” acting secretary Hung Cao posted on X after an incident Wednesday involving an F/A-18 that one observer estimated flew about 90 feet over his head.

item.title: How Trump became the accidental green president
item.description: How Trump became the accidental green president

item.title: Trump approval stuck in the 30s amid pessimism on Iran and economy, Washington Post-Ipsos poll finds
item.description: Americans give the president broadly negative assessments, and support has waned among many Republicans.

item.title: More than 75 people rescued, 1 killed as rain deluges areas of Texas
item.description: The death occurred because of flooding in the Guadalupe River, Gov. Greg Abbott said, in the same region that experienced a deadly event in July 2025.

item.title: The Vatican says these breakaway Catholics are sinning. Many are defiant.
item.description: The newly excommunicated Society of St. Pius X has established an outpost of traditionalism on the Kansas prairie.

item.title: ‘Throw it all out’: I inherited my parents’ stuff — and a painful decision
item.description: Mom’s samovar? Dad’s chess set? Things from the past are company, not clutter.

item.title: Reflecting Pool peeling probably caused by application flaws, a Post analysis finds
item.description: President Donald Trump has blamed vandals, but a visual examination found that the peeling tracks closely with stages of the renovation process.

item.title: ‘Maybe the T.rex’ in today’s On the Record
item.description: ‘Maybe the T.rex’ in today’s On the Record

item.title: Wildfire smoke will worsen in the Northeast and Mid-Atlantic through Friday
item.description: More than 115 million people could be exposed to unhealthy air quality. See the forecast for 20 cities.

item.title: Trump poll. ICE pivot. Israel vote. World Cup. Smoke forecast. Cyclosporiasis. Dopamine sites. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: The 25 most influential works of American culture
item.description: A decade-by-decade look at the books, music, art and ideas that shaped society.

item.title: House vote over military aid exposes Democrats’ widening divide over Israel
item.description: Wednesday’s symbolic vote showed how far Democratic politics on Israel have shifted, as progressive candidates turn anger over Gaza and U.S. military aid into a campaign issue.

item.title: Argentina rallies in dramatic second half to defeat England, 2-1, will face Spain in World Cup final
item.description: Argentina trailed England well into the final minutes, but two assists from Lionel Messi lifted the team to yet another comeback as the defending champions scored in the 85th and 93rd minutes.

item.title: Nolan’s gorgeous ‘Odyssey’ makes the controversies evaporate
item.description: Nolan’s gorgeous ‘Odyssey’ makes the controversies evaporate

item.title: How scientists tracked down a new monkey species in the Congo rainforest
item.description: The “Likweli” marks the second species discovered in the past 15 years around the Lomami River, in a remote and thickly forested part of the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: Wildfire smoke from Canada is spreading across the Midwest and East
item.description: Around 100 million people in the U.S. may face unhealthy air quality. See where smoke is headed.

item.title: Trump’s pick for intelligence chief says he is not an ‘election denier’ but refuses to say Biden ‘won’ in 2020
item.description: Jay Clayton appeared before a Senate committee weeks after President Donald Trump derailed the originally scheduled confirmation hearing. Clayton is nominated to replace acting director of national intelligence Bill Pulte, a mortgage agency chief with no prior national security experience.

item.title: U.S. Mint to produce $1 coin with Trump’s face on it, treasury secretary says
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced the coin will commemorate America’s 250th birthday. Its face features Trump alongside the phrase “In God We Trust.” It is encircled with the phrases “Liberty” and “1776-2026.” The flip-side of the coin shows an eagle holding arrows in one talon and olive branches in the other.
